title: odontites vernus subsp. serotinus
intro: Odontites vernus subsp. serotinus, also known as the late-toothed odontites, is a plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by tapping into the vascular tissue of nearby host plants, typically grasses and sedges. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
